California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Sabina Gilbert 
511 5th Street 
Lincoln, CA 
95648 
Dear Ms. Gilbert: 
September 11, 1989 
Re: 
Your Request for Informal Assistance 
Our File No. 1-89-298 
This is in response to your request for guidance regarding 
your filing obligations under the provisions of the Political 
Reform Act (the "Act").l 
Since your question does not involve a 
specific pending decision, we are treating your request as one for 
informal assistance pursuant to Regulation 18329(c) (copy 
enclosed) .2 
QUESTION 
Which form do you need to file as your annual statement of 
economic interests, in your role as special counsel to the City of 
Lincoln, Form 721 or Form 730? 
CONCLUSION 
In your role as special counsel to the City of Lincoln, you 
may be deemed a "consultant" within the meaning of the Act. 
The 
conflict of interest code of the city would specify the filing and 
disclosure obligations of a consultant. 
Any financial interests 
you are required to disclose would be disclosed on your annual 
statement of economic interests by filing Form 730. 
1 
Government Code Sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory references 
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. 
Commission 
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 
18000, et seq. 
All references to regulations are to Title 2, 
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 
2 
Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the 
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. 
(Government Code Section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs. Section 
18329 (c) (3) • ) 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804#0807 • (916)322#5660 


Our File No. I-89-298 
Page 2 
FACTS 
You have been retained as special counsel (hereafter 
"counsel") by the City of Lincoln (hereafter "city") by an 
agreement dated February 11, 1988. 
As counsel you provided legal 
advice to the city concerning preparation of an amendment to the 
general plan. 
You expected your duties to consist of telephone 
consultation and preparation of documents subsequent to staff's 
negotiating such documents. 
However, during the course of your 
duties you have been involved in protracted negotiations over a 
developer reimbursement agreement you were asked to prepare. 
You 
have done virtually nothing since the conclusion of those 
negotiations. 
You have filed a statement of economic interests, Form 721, 
disclosing your economic interests. 
By telephone we have advised 
you that you are not required to file Form 721, but that you might 
be required to file Form 730 instead. 
ANALYSIS 
section 87200, et seq. provides that certain specified 
elected and appointed public officials, and candidates for those 
positions, must file statements of economic interests. 
Those 
officials must file Form 721. 
The city positions covered by 
section 87200 are mayor, city manager, city attorney, chief 
administrative officer and city council members. 
Section 87300 provides that every agency shall adopt a 
conflict of interest code. 
In its conflict of interest code, each 
agency must designate those positions that involve the making of, 
or participation in, decisions which may foreseeably affect any 
financial interest. 
(Section 87302.) 
Both the definitions of 
"designated employee" and "public official" in the Act include any 
consultants to an agency who make or participate in such agency 
decisions. 3 
(Sections 82019 and 82048.) 
3 
A "consultant," within the meaning of the Act, includes "any 
natural person who provides, under contract, information, advice, 
recommendation or counsel to a state or local government 
agency .... 11 
(Regulation 18700(A) (2).) 
This term does not 
include, however, a person who: 
(A) 
Conducts research and arrives at 
conclusions with respect to his or her rendition of 
information, advice, recommendation or counsel 
independent of the control and direction of the 
agency or of any agency official, other than normal 
contract monitoring; and 
(B) 
Possesses no authority with respect to 
any agency decision beyond the rendition of 


Our File No. 1-89-298 
Page 3 
A consultant is not included in Section 87200 as one of the 
persons required to file a statement of economic interests 
pursuant to that section. 
Accordingly, a consultant, such as 
yourself, would file a statement of economic interests pursuant to 
a conflict of interest code adopted by the city. 
We do not have a 
copy of the conflict of interest code adopted by the city. 
You 
should check that conflict of interest code to determine your 
filing and disclosure obligations. 
However, in general, all city 
public officials listed in the conflict of interest code, except 
those specified in Section 87200, would file Form 730 with the 
city as their annual statement of economic interests. 
Please note that there are some differences in the 
information that would be disclosed on Form 721 and Form 730. 
For 
example, Form 730 requires disclosure of "business positions" on 
Schedule G, but no such disclosure is required on Form 721. 
Also, 
as discussed above, the extent of disclosure required by 
consultants and other designated employees under a conflict of 
interest code may be more limited than the disclosure required on 
Form 721 pursuant to Section 87200. 
I trust this letter has provided you with the guidance you 
requested. 
If you have any further questions regarding this 
matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5901. 
KED/JSA/aa 
Enclosures 
(Continue footnote #3) 
Sincerely, 
Kathryn E. Donovan 
Genera~unsel 
BY:~;~~ 
Counsel, Legal Division 
information, advice, recommendation or counsel. 
Regulation 18700(a) (2) (A)-(B). 
When an attorney is hired under a contract to perform legal 
services for a specific matter (e.g., a particular piece of 
litigation), he or she ordinarily comes within this exemption. 
Essentially, the attorney is being called upon to deliver a 
finished product and-not to participate in or advise a city on 
general governmental decisions requiring legal expertise. 
On the 
other hand, when an attorney provides general advice and counsel 
to the city on a continuing basis, it is the commission's view 
that the attorney must file the appropriate statements of economic 
interests. 
(See, e.g., Frederick Advice Letter, No. 1-86-047; 
Albuquerque Advice letter, No. A-85-244; copies enclosed.)

Practices Commission 
Sabina D. Gilbert 
Special Legal Counsel 
511 -
5th Street 
Lincoln, CA 
95648 
Dear Ms. Gilbert: 
May 23, 1989 
Re: 
Letter No. 89-298 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act 
was received on May 18, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices 
Commission. 
If you have any questions about your advice request, 
you may contact John McLean an attorney in the Legal Division, 
directly at (916) 322-5901. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or 
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21 
working days if your request seeks formal written advice. 
If more 
information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response 
to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to 
information needed. 
If your request is for informal assistance, 
we will answer it as quickly as we can. 
(See Commission 
Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).) 
You also should be aware that your letter and our response 
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon 
receipt of a proper request for disclosure. 
KED:plh 
Very truly yours, 
;Li!! 
Kathryn E. Donovan 
General Counsel 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Dear Filing Officer: 
~h '89 
TELEPHONE MS-3314 
May 1, 1989 
Enclosed are two Form 721's that I am submitting in my 
capacity as Special Counsel to the City of Lincoln. 
One is an 
assuming office statement and the other is the financial interest 
disclosure statement for 1988. 
I ask that you accept these 
statements and waive any late fines for the reasons set out 
below. 
I was retained by the City of Lincoln as Special Counsel by 
agreement dated February 11, 1988. 
As Special Counsel I was 
asked to provide legal advice to the City concerning the 
preparation of an amendment to the City's General plan. 
The City 
Attorney was disqualified from advising the City on this project 
due to a conflict of interest. 
I served as City Attorney for the City of Lincoln from 1981 
to 1983. 
Based on that prior experience, I expected my role as 
Special Counsel to involve limited contacts with the staff and 
Council. 
I expected to be consulted primarily by telephone and 
to be asked to prepare documents (such as agreements, ordinances, 
and resolutions) after the substance of each had been negotiated 
or otherwise worked out by staff. 
As such, I never thought of 
the need to file Form 721. 
My work for the City during the first few months under the 
agreement consisted of attending one meeting to discuss the scope 
of the project. 
On or about May 1988 I was asked to prepare a 
developer reimbursement agreement. 
After discusing the basic 
provisions with the City Administrator over the telephone, I 
prepared a draft and returned it, expecting only minimal changes. 
In fact, however, I soon became deeply involved in protracted 
negotiations over this agreement which lasted through the end of 
1988. 
Since the conclusion of those negotiations I have done 
virtually nothing. 


Filing Officer 
Fait Political Practices 
Commission 
Page 2 
May 1, 1989 
Late in the year I started to reconsider the need to file 
Form 721, because the work I was doing was beginning to look more 
and more like that of a "consultant" within the meaning of the 
law. 
Early in 1989 I discussed my situation with one of your 
staff attorneys, Margaret Ellison. 
With her input and after 
reviewing a number of advice letters on this issue which she 
provided me, I have concluded that my status as Special Counsel 
cannot be definitively categorized one way or another. 
Rather 
than continue researching and wondering about the question, I 
have opted to simply file the disclosure statements, and have 
used the date of my agreement with the City of Lincoln as the 
date of assuming office. 
Please accept the enclosed forms for filing and waive any 
late fines. 
If you wish to discuss this with me you may reach me 
at (916) 624-3351 or (916) 428-1815. 
Thank you. 
SDG:ms 
Enclosures (2) 
Sincerely, 
0~]).~ 
Sabina D. Gilbert 
Special Legal Counsel

sections.question:
Which form do you need to file as your annual statement of 
economic interests, in your role as special counsel to the City of 
Lincoln, Form 721 or Form 730?
sections.conclusion:
In your role as special counsel to the City of Lincoln, you 
may be deemed a "consultant" within the meaning of the Act. 
The 
conflict of interest code of the city would specify the filing and 
disclosure obligations of a consultant. 
Any financial interests 
you are required to disclose would be disclosed on your annual 
statement of economic interests by filing Form 730. 
, et seq. 
All references to regulations are to Title 2, 
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 
2 
Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the 
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. 
(Government Code Section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs. Section 
18329 (c) (3) • ) 
#0807 • (916)322#5660 


Our Page 2
sections.facts:
You have been retained as special counsel (hereafter 
"counsel") by the City of Lincoln (hereafter "city") by an 
agreement dated February 11, 1988. 
As counsel you provided legal 
advice to the city concerning preparation of an amendment to the 
general plan. 
You expected your duties to consist of telephone 
consultation and preparation of documents subsequent to staff's 
negotiating such documents. 
However, during the course of your 
duties you have been involved in protracted negotiations over a 
developer reimbursement agreement you were asked to prepare. 
You 
have done virtually nothing since the conclusion of those 
negotiations. 
You have filed a statement of economic interests, Form 721, 
disclosing your economic interests. 
By telephone we have advised 
you that you are not required to file Form 721, but that you might 
be required to file Form 730 instead.
sections.analysis:
section 87200, et seq. provides that certain specified 
elected and appointed public officials, and candidates for those 
positions, must file statements of economic interests. 
Those 
officials must file Form 721. 
The city positions covered by 
section 87200 are mayor, city manager, city attorney, chief 
administrative officer and city council members. 
Section 87300 provides that every agency shall adopt a 
conflict of interest code. 
In its conflict of interest code, each 
agency must designate those positions that involve the making of, 
or participation in, decisions which may foreseeably affect any 
financial interest. 
(Section 87302.) 
Both the definitions of 
"designated employee" and "public official" in the Act include any 
consultants to an agency who make or participate in such agency 
decisions. 3 
(Sections 82019 and 82048.) 
3 
A "consultant," within the meaning of the Act, includes "any 
natural person who provides, under contract, information, advice, 
recommendation or counsel to a state or local government 
agency .... 11 
(Regulation 18700(A) (2).) 
This term does not 
include, however, a person who: 
(A) 
Conducts research and arrives at 
conclusions with respect to his or her rendition of 
information, advice, recommendation or counsel 
independent of the control and direction of the 
agency or of any agency official, other than normal 
contract monitoring; and 
(B) 
Possesses no authority with respect to 
any agency decision beyond the rendition of 


Our Page 3 
A consultant is not included in Section 87200 as one of the 
persons required to file a statement of economic interests 
pursuant to that section. 
Accordingly, a consultant, such as 
yourself, would file a statement of economic interests pursuant to 
a conflict of interest code adopted by the city. 
We do not have a 
copy of the conflict of interest code adopted by the city. 
You 
should check that conflict of interest code to determine your 
filing and disclosure obligations. 
However, in general, all city 
public officials listed in the conflict of interest code, except 
those specified in Section 87200, would file Form 730 with the 
city as their annual statement of economic interests. 
Please note that there are some differences in the 
information that would be disclosed on Form 721 and Form 730. 
For 
example, Form 730 requires disclosure of "business positions" on 
Schedule G, but no such disclosure is required on Form 721. 
Also, 
as discussed above, the extent of disclosure required by 
consultants and other designated employees under a conflict of 
interest code may be more limited than the disclosure required on 
Form 721 pursuant to Section 87200. 
I trust this letter has provided you with the guidance you 
requested.

economic interests, in your role as special counsel to the City of

CONCLUSION: In your role as special counsel to the City of Lincoln, you 
may be deemed a "consultant" within the meaning of the Act. 
The 
conflict of interest code of the city would specify the filing and 
disclosure obligations of a consultant. 
Any financial interests 
you are required to disclose would be disclosed on your annual 
statement of economic interests by filing Form 730.

embedding.first_500_words: California Fair Political Practices Commission Sabina Gilbert 511 5th Street Lincoln, CA 95648 Dear Ms. Gilbert: September 11, 1989 Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance Our File No. 1-89-298 This is in response to your request for guidance regarding your filing obligations under the provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").l Since your question does not involve a specific pending decision, we are treating your request as one for informal assistance pursuant to Regulation 18329(c) (copy enclosed) .2 QUESTION Which form do you need to file as your annual statement of economic interests, in your role as special counsel to the City of Lincoln, Form 721 or Form 730? CONCLUSION In your role as special counsel to the City of Lincoln, you may be deemed a "consultant" within the meaning of the Act. The conflict of interest code of the city would specify the filing and disclosure obligations of a consultant. Any financial interests you are required to disclose would be disclosed on your annual statement of economic interests by filing Form 730. 1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. (Government Code Section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs. Section 18329 (c) (3) • ) 428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804#0807 • (916)322#5660 Our File No. I-89-298 Page 2 FACTS You have been retained as special counsel (hereafter "counsel") by the City of Lincoln (hereafter "city") by an agreement dated February 11, 1988. As counsel you provided legal advice to the city concerning preparation of an amendment to the general plan. You expected your duties to consist of telephone consultation and preparation of documents subsequent to staff's negotiating such documents. However, during the course of your duties you have been involved in protracted negotiations over a developer reimbursement agreement you were asked to prepare. You have done virtually nothing since the conclusion of those negotiations. You have filed a statement of economic interests, Form 721, disclosing your economic interests. By telephone we have advised you that you are not required to file Form 721, but that you might be required to file Form 730 instead. ANALYSIS section 87200, et seq. provides that certain specified elected and appointed public officials, and candidates for those positions, must file statements of economic interests. Those officials must file Form 721. The city positions covered by section 87200 are mayor, city manager, city attorney, chief administrative officer and city council members. Section 87300 provides that every agency shall adopt a conflict of interest code. In its conflict of interest code, each agency must designate those positions that involve the making of, or participation in, decisions which may foreseeably affect any
